pages.raw_text:
HALTEMAN CONFIDENT THAT


EXHIBIT AT A. Y. p. FAIR


WILL BE GREAT EDUCATOR


IN VALUE OF NATIVE RE-


SOURCES.


w. A. Halteman of Ferry county,
who has charge Of the Washington
mining exhibit at the A. Y. P.
worlds fair next year, is in the
city, comforting with L. P. Horn
berger, one of the fair commission
ers for the state. He will be here
over Sunday.


Mining men of Washington
have just awakened to a realiza-
ton of the Importance of a large
and comprehensive exhibit of the
mining resources of the state"
said Mr.. Halteman. People of our
own state do not even know the
extent of our development along
this line. The state of Washington
possesses some of the largest de-
posits of low grade ores in the
northwest, which have not been
extensively developed into paying
properties as yet, because capital
has sought other fields that looked
more inviting.


Heavy investments of capital
are needed to develop mining, and
the only way we can get men of
means interested is to show them
that we have the goods. l am con-
fident that If mining exhibit such
as we are planning will be made
it will return to the state in two
or three years time in additional
mining investments, all that the
entire exposition will cost.


There are over so distinct min
ing districts in the state, repre-
senting localities in which precious
metals have been found in guam
titles sufficient to warrant develop
ment. Nearly all of these districts
will be represented at the exposi-
ton by an exhibit so complete that
mining man can tell at a glance
what the district contains. In over
rs of these districts mining men
have consented to act as deputies
to aid in assembling exhibits"
